introSection: Boomerang is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Worlds of Fun in Kansas City, Missouri, USA. Opened in 2000, it is situated in the Africa section of the park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On December 7, 1999, Worlds of Fun announced that they would be adding Boomerang. It opened to guests on April 8, 2000. The ride replaced the original Zambezi Zinger and previously used the former ride's queue line and entrance.

In 2023, the ride received a new paint job and queue theming.

A new extended queue area was added for the 2025 season.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This is a standard Boomerang installation from Vekoma.

fullText:
Boomerang is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Worlds of Fun in Kansas City, Missouri, USA. Opened in 2000, it is situated in the Africa section of the park.

On December 7, 1999, Worlds of Fun announced that they would be adding Boomerang. It opened to guests on April 8, 2000. The ride replaced the original Zambezi Zinger and previously used the former ride's queue line and entrance.

In 2023, the ride received a new paint job and queue theming.

A new extended queue area was added for the 2025 season.

This is a standard Boomerang installation from Vekoma.

Red track and yellow supports.
